name: Dijon Rstd Loin Pork W/Apple Cider Sauce
recipeIngredient: 5 lbs trimmed, boned, rolled, and tied pork loin
3 1/4 lbs Macintosh or Granny Smith apples, sliced into 1/4-inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Rub the exterior of the pork loin with sage and coriander.
3. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat.
4. Sear the pork roast on all sides until browned (about 2-3 minutes per side).
5. Transfer the roast to a baking rack inside a roasting pan.
6. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for about 1.5 hours or until the internal temperature reaches 150 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. Remove from the oven and let rest.
8. In the same skillet, sear the apples and onions over medium heat until caramelized (about 10-15 minutes).
9. Deglaze the pan with Madeira wine, scraping up any browned bits.
10. Add apple cider, mustard, and evaporated milk to the pan.
11. Reduce mixture by half, stirring frequently (about 5-7 minutes).
12. Slice the pork into serving portions.
13. Spoon sauce over the pork.
14. Garnish with sliced apples and onions and fresh sage leaves.
